prerequisites: A Poplar account at app.heypoplar.com with access to the API credentials page.

An access token (Test or Production) generated from your Poplar account's API credentials page. Production tokens are required for endpoints beyond mailings; Test tokens only work for the mailings endpoint.
A start date (the point in time from which the connector begins reading records).

authMethods.name: Access token (Bearer)
authMethods.summary: Poplar's API uses bearer token authorization. The connector's access_token secret field is sent as an Authorization: Bearer <access token> header on each request. Generate the token from the API credentials page in your Poplar account (app.heypoplar.com/credentials), choosing a Test or Production token. Treat the token like a password; it can be revoked from the credentials page if compromised.

setupSteps.title: Generate a Poplar access token
setupSteps.body: Sign in to your Poplar account and open the API credentials page at app.heypoplar.com/credentials. Create an access token: a Production token works across all endpoints and sends real mailings, while a Test token (identified by a test prefix) only works for the mailings endpoint and does not actually send. Copy the token; it grants access to private account data, so store it securely.

setupSteps.title: Provide the access_token
setupSteps.body: Enter the generated token in the connector's required access_token field. The connector sends it as an Authorization: Bearer header against the Poplar API base URL https://api.heypoplar.com/v1, alongside an Accept: application/json header.

setupSteps.title: Set the start date
setupSteps.body: Enter a value in the required start_date field. This marks the point in time from which the connector begins reading records, so set it to the earliest date you want data synced from.
